chapter: ENG9_8 – A House Is Not a Home
title: A House Is Not a Home – Medium SAQs

questions.question: Why did the author feel uncomfortable during his first year of high school?
questions.answer: The author felt uncomfortable because the school was much bigger and his old friends had gone to another school.

questions.question: How did the author's old teachers try to help him?
questions.answer: They encouraged him to take part in school activities so that he could meet new people and adjust to the new school.

questions.question: Describe the author's relationship with his cat.
questions.answer: The author loved his red tabby cat deeply and the cat always stayed close to him.

questions.question: What signs showed that the house was on fire?
questions.answer: Smoke started coming through the ceiling and quickly filled the room.

questions.question: Why did the author's mother risk her life during the fire?
questions.answer: She wanted to save the important documents and photographs of her late husband.

questions.question: How did the firefighters help the author and his mother?
questions.answer: They rescued the author's mother and comforted the author by wrapping him in a blanket.

questions.question: Why did the author feel helpless while watching the fire?
questions.answer: He could only watch as his house and belongings were destroyed.

questions.question: What was the author’s biggest worry after the fire?
questions.answer: He was worried that his cat might have died in the fire.

questions.question: Why did the author go to school even after the tragedy?
questions.answer: His mother insisted that he continue his normal routine and attend school.

questions.question: Why did the author feel embarrassed at school?
questions.answer: He had lost his clothes, books, and backpack in the fire.

questions.question: Why did the author say he walked around like a zombie?
questions.answer: He was shocked and emotionally numb after losing everything.

questions.question: What items were saved from the house?
questions.answer: Photo albums and important documents were saved.

questions.question: Why did the author often visit the ruins of his house?
questions.answer: He hoped to find his missing cat there.

questions.question: How did the classmates show kindness to the author?
questions.answer: They collected clothes, notebooks, and school supplies for him.

questions.question: Why was the author surprised by his classmates’ actions?
questions.answer: Many of the students helping him had never spoken to him before.

questions.question: What effect did the classmates’ kindness have on the author?
questions.answer: It made him feel accepted and helped him make new friends.

questions.question: Why did the author begin to feel hopeful again?
questions.answer: Because he received support and friendship from others.

questions.question: What was happening to the author's house a month later?
questions.answer: It was being rebuilt.

questions.question: Why did the author say his life was being rebuilt?
questions.answer: Because he had found new friends and confidence.

questions.question: How was the author's cat finally returned?
questions.answer: A kind woman who found the cat brought it back.

questions.question: Why had the cat run away from the house?
questions.answer: It ran away because it was frightened by the fire.

questions.question: How did the author react when he got his cat back?
questions.answer: He hugged the cat and cried with happiness.

questions.question: What lesson did the author learn from the tragedy?
questions.answer: He learned the importance of kindness and friendship.

questions.question: What does the rebuilding of the house represent?
questions.answer: It represents the rebuilding of the author’s life.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: Human kindness and relationships make life meaningful.
